name: Bank Michigan
description_short: Southeast Michigan community bank offering checking, savings, CDs, mortgages, and business loans with over 100 years of local presence and FDIC insurance.
description_long:
Bank Michigan has operated as a community bank in Southeast Michigan since its founding in 1907, establishing itself as a local financial institution with deep roots in the region. The bank serves both personal and business customers through multiple physical locations in communities including Onsted, Brooklyn, and Manchester, MI, with plans for continued expansion.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business banking products. On the personal side, customers can access multiple checking account options (Advantage, Clearly Free, 50 or Better, High Interest Platinum, Next Generation), savings accounts, money market accounts, CDs, IRAs, mortgages, home equity loans, consumer loans, credit cards, and HSAs. Business customers have access to business checking, SBA lending, commercial real estate financing, term loans, lines of credit, merchant services, treasury management, and specialized cannabis lending programs.

Bank Michigan differentiates itself through emphasis on personalized service and community involvement. The website highlights personal bankers, one-to-one coffee consultations, automatic greetings upon branch visits, and a 24/7 CEO hotline. The bank emphasizes its century-long commitment to the local community, organized community events, and charitable giving through programs like the Community Care Scholarship. All deposits are FDIC-insured, providing federal backing.

As a regional community bank, Bank Michigan is most suitable for customers prioritizing local relationship banking, face-to-face service, and community involvement over digital-first or high-yield commodity products. The institution appears well-capitalized and legitimate, though specific APY rates, fee structures, and loan terms require direct inquiry. Customers seeking extensive branch networks or premium yields on savings products may find larger national banks more competitive. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking accounts (Advantage, Clearly Free, 50 or Better, High Interest Platinum, Next Generation)
Business checking including government and school accounts
Savings accounts and youth savings accounts
SBA lending and commercial real estate financing
Business lines of credit and term loans
Online and mobile banking with Zelle payments
Specialized cannabis lending for qualified businesses
pros: Over 100 years of established local community banking presence in Southeast Michigan
FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Multiple personal checking options including service charge-free checking
Comprehensive business lending including SBA loans, commercial real estate, and cannabis lending
Personal service features including one-to-one coffee consultations with bankers and 24/7 CEO hotline
Full-service offerings from basic checking to mortgages, investment products, and merchant services
Active community involvement with charity partnerships and scholarship programs for students
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Southeast Michigan may not serve customers in other regions
Website does not display specific APY rates, fee structures, or loan terms—requires direct contact
No mention of premium high-yield savings rates or competitive CD yields compared to national banks
Digital banking capabilities mentioned but not prominently featured compared to neobanks and larger regional competitors
No information provided about minimum account balances, monthly fees for checking accounts, or overdraft policies
best_for: Southeast Michigan residents and businesses seeking relationship-based banking with local decision-making
Small business owners and entrepreneurs needing personalized lending solutions including SBA loans
Community-focused customers who value local charitable involvement and direct access to bank leadership
Customers prioritizing branch-based services and face-to-face financial consultation over digital-only banking
diagnosis: Bank Michigan is best for Southeast Michigan residents and businesses seeking personalized, relationship-driven community banking with local branch presence and decision-making authority. The primary caveat is that as a regional community bank, it has limited geographic reach and does not publicly display competitive rates or fees online—customers must contact branches directly for pricing on checking, savings, CDs, and loan products.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
